intro: The biggest real split between Apify and Apache APISIX is lock-in posture: Apify carries medium lock-in (actors portable as docker, results exportable), while Apache APISIX carries low lock-in (apache oss api gateway). That single fact shapes the rest of the comparison. Apify is built for data warehouse syncing and is not a fit for simple, small projects. Apache APISIX is built for data warehouse syncing and is not a fit for simple, small projects. On the scoring side, Apify rates 5/5 for developer experience and 4/5 for transparency; Apache APISIX rates 5/5 and 4/5 respectively. The honest trade-off: with Apify you accept that simple api-to-api integrations, while Apache APISIX comes with the reality that simple api-to-api integrations. Neither side is free. Apify's headline strength in the data is that data warehouse syncing. Apache APISIX's headline strength is that data warehouse syncing. Read each side's best-for and not-for fields carefully before you pick; the shape of your team and workflow matters more than any single score.
quick_take: Apify is for data warehouse syncing; Apache APISIX is for data warehouse syncing; decide based on lock-in tolerance.
verdict_a: Choose Apify if you are data warehouse syncing and can accept medium lock-in (actors portable as docker, results exportable).
verdict_b: Choose Apache APISIX if you are data warehouse syncing and need low lock-in (apache oss api gateway).

faq.q: Is Apify cheaper than Apache APISIX?
faq.a: Both list a freemium pricing model in our data, and both publish a free tier. We don't have per-seat numbers in this dataset, so 'cheaper' depends on volume. Price both on your actual usage before deciding.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Apify to Apache APISIX?
faq.a: Apify has medium lock-in (actors portable as docker, results exportable); Apache APISIX has low lock-in (apache oss api gateway). Migration effort scales with how much state and automation you've built in Apify; plan for data export, config rebuild, and a parallel-run period before cutting over.
Both Apify and Apache APISIX score 5/5 on developer experience in our data, so DX isn't the tiebreaker here. Decide on fit, pricing, or lock-in instead.

faq.q: Is Apache APISIX a good alternative to Apify?
faq.a: Apache APISIX is built for data warehouse syncing and explicitly not for simple, small projects, so it's a fit only if your workflow matches its stated audience. If you were using Apify for data warehouse syncing, the switch is reasonable; outside that scope, look elsewhere.
